questions.question: Define Data Structure and explain its importance in computer science.
keyPoints: Data structure is a specialized way of organizing and storing data
Enables efficient access and modification of data
Choice of data structure impacts algorithm performance
Fundamental building block of software engineering

questions.question: Differentiate between Primitive and Non-Primitive Data Structures with examples.
keyPoints: Primitive are basic types operated by machine instructions (int, float, char)
Non-Primitive are derived from primitive types
Non-Primitive includes Linear (array, list) and Non-Linear (tree, graph)
Examples: Primitive=int, Non-Primitive=array, linked list

questions.question: Explain the four fundamental operations performed on data structures.
keyPoints: Traversing: visiting each element once
Searching: finding location of element
Insertion: adding new element
Deletion: removing existing element

questions.question: What is a Sparse Matrix? Explain its representation with an example.
keyPoints: Sparse matrix has most elements as zero
Storing all elements wastes memory
Represented by storing only non-zero elements with positions
Triplet form: (row, column, value) for each non-zero element

questions.question: Explain the difference between Static and Dynamic Memory Allocation.
keyPoints: Static: fixed memory at compile time, on stack
Dynamic: variable memory at runtime, on heap
Dynamic uses malloc/calloc/new functions
Dynamic allows flexible memory management but requires explicit deallocation

questions.question: Define Structure and Union. How do they differ in memory allocation?
keyPoints: Structure: each member has separate memory location sequentially
Union: all members share same memory location
Union size equals largest member size
Structure size is sum of all member sizes

questions.question: Explain Row-Major and Column-Major representation of 2D arrays with formula.
keyPoints: Row-Major: elements stored row by row in memory
Column-Major: elements stored column by column
Row-Major address = Base + (i×n + j)×size
Column-Major address = Base + (j×m + i)×size

questions.question: Explain the concept of Arrays as a data structure. Discuss different types of arrays with their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Arrays: collection of elements of same type at contiguous memory locations
Static arrays: fixed size declared at compile time
Dynamic arrays: size can change at runtime
Advantages: O(1) access time, simple implementation, cache-friendly
Disadvantages: fixed size (static), expensive insertion/deletion at beginning

questions.question: Explain the representation of polynomials using arrays. Write an algorithm to add two polynomials represented as arrays.
keyPoints: Polynomial represented with array index as exponent and value as coefficient
Example: 3x² + 2x + 5 stored as [5, 2, 3]
Addition: add coefficients of corresponding exponents
Algorithm: traverse both arrays, add coefficients at same index, handle different degrees

questions.question: Discuss Pointers and Dynamic Memory Allocation in C. Explain malloc(), calloc(), and free() with examples.
keyPoints: Pointer stores memory address of another variable
malloc(): allocates specified bytes, returns void pointer
calloc(): allocates and initializes to zero
free(): deallocates previously allocated memory
Always check for NULL return, always free memory to avoid leaks

questions.question: Explain the need for Sparse Matrix representation. Discuss any two methods for representing sparse matrices with their advantages.
keyPoints: Sparse matrices have most elements as zero
Storing all elements wastes significant memory
Triplet representation: stores (row, column, value) for each non-zero
Linked representation: uses nodes with row, column, value, and next pointer
Triplet: simple, efficient for small k. Linked: efficient for dynamic operations
